title: Michael B. Jordan Wins Best Actor at 2026 Oscars - E! Online
text: Michael B. Jordan claimed his first Academy Award at the 2026 Oscars ceremony on March 15, triumphing in the Best Actor category for his dual roles as twin brothers in Sinners. During his remarks at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles, Jordan expressed gratitude to supporters, stating: Thank you, everybody in this room and everybody at home, for supporting me over my career. He also highlighted his father's presence at the event, noting: My dad came in from Ghana to be here. Historic Achievement: The 39-year-old's victory makes him the sixth Black performer to win Best Actor, joining Sidney Poitier, Denzel Washington, Jamie Foxx, Forest Whitaker, and Will Smith. He acknowledged these predecessors and Halle Berry as giants in the industry. Award Season Momentum: Jordan had built momentum heading into Oscar night, having won at the Actor Awards two weeks prior. He credited the collaborative atmosphere on set, describing the experience with director Ryan Coogler: It's a family environment. The film Sinners also won for Original Screenplay, Original Score, Cinematography, and Visual Effects.
